name: Web Workflows
description: Standard for a workflow — a set of smaller UX intents arranged in directed progression toward one goal (checkout = collect-address → pay → confirm), with threaded state, a single completion criterion, and one current position. The missing third composition mode (after simultaneous and cross-cutting): directed/temporal composition of step-intents. It defines the Workflow Protocol — the orchestration graph (steps + transitions + guards + parallel/fork-join + completion) as a portable, data-defined SCXML-style interchange, resolved by a swappable CustomWorkflowEngine registry (XState, SCION, a hand-rolled switch plug in behind it), emitting a step-transition event. The UX (current position, per-step status, register) is the Flow Progress intent; the concrete wizard is a Block. Distinct sibling of Web Lifecycle: a workflow is the user's ephemeral traversal (process orchestration, may occupy many states), not a persisted entity's status (an FSM, one state). Graduated from #634 (A / A ratified); subsumes #369.
